name: Senda del Lince – circular to the Castrola cave (PR-TO 11)
name_original: Senda del Lince – circular a la cueva Castrola (PR-TO 11)
region_intro: Hiking around Madridejos offers exploration amidst a serene environment, characterized by a hilly and rolling topography with open grasslands and second-growth forests. The region features fairly level and rolling plateaus and valleys suitable for upland walks. Coastal areas provide opportunities for strolls along white sand beaches and baywalks, including the Omagieca Obo-ob Mangrove Garden. The average elevation is low, with a maximum of 25 meters, indicating that "hiking" here refers to exploring varied natural terrains rather than ascending high peaks. Best hiking trails around Madridejos The most popular hiking route is Senda del Lince – circular to the Castrola cave (PR-TO 11), an 8.6 miles (13.9 km) trail that takes 3 hours 53 minutes to complete. This route is rated as moderate. Another top favourite among local hikers is Consuegra Castle and Windmills Trail, an easy 3.0 miles (4.8 km) path. It offers a relatively short walk suitable for various fitness levels. Local hikers also love the Calderico Hill – Consuegra Castle loop from Consuegra, a 2.1 miles (3.4 km) trail, often completed in about 58 minutes. This easy route provides a quick outdoor experience. Hiking around Madridejos is defined by coastal paths, rolling terrain, and mangrove forests. The network offers options for different ability levels, from leisurely walks to moderate trails. The routes in Madridejos are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 50 reviews. More than 270 hikers have used komoot to explore Madridejos's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes around Madridejos?
A: Hiking around Madridejos primarily features a serene environment with a hilly and rolling topography, open grasslands, and second-growth forests. You'll find fairly level and rolling plateaus and valleys suitable for upland walks. Coastal areas offer opportunities for strolls along white sand beaches and baywalks, including the unique Omagieca Obo-ob Mangrove Garden. The elevation is generally low, with a maximum of 25 meters, meaning hikes here are more about exploring varied natural terrains and scenic viewpoints rather than strenuous mountain ascents.

Q: Are there many hiking trails available in Madridejos?
A: Yes, Madridejos offers a good selection of outdoor paths. There are over 30 hiking routes available, catering to various preferences and fitness levels. These include easy walks, moderate trails, and a few more challenging options.

Q: Are there any easy, family-friendly walks in Madridejos?
A: Absolutely. Madridejos has 14 easy routes perfect for families or those seeking a relaxed stroll. One popular easy option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1019744256/consuegra-castle-and-windmills-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Consuegra Castle and Windmills Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which is 3.0 miles (4.8 km) long. Another great choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10372548" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Calderico Hill – Consuegra Castle loop from Consuegra&lt;/a&gt;, a 2.1 miles (3.4 km) trail that can be completed in under an hour.

Q: What are some notable natural features or landmarks I can see while hiking?
A: Madridejos offers several interesting natural features and landmarks. You can explore the unique Omagieca Obo-ob Mangrove Garden, which provides insights into the vital role mangroves play in the ecosystem. Coastal walks offer expansive views of the Visayan Sea. For historical interest, Kota Park features a baywalk and the ruins of an old Spanish fort, with a watch tower offering elevated views. Nearby, you can also find natural monuments like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3902682" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Calderico Hill&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Madridejos?
A: Yes, there are several circular routes available. A highly-rated moderate option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1018339598/senda-del-lince-circular-to-the-castrola-cave-pr-to-11" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Senda del Lince – circular to the Castrola cave (PR-TO 11)&lt;/a&gt;, which is 8.6 miles (13.9 km) long. Another easy circular path is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31429157" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Consuegra Castle – Windmills of Consuegra loop from Consuegra&lt;/a&gt;, covering 2.2 miles (3.5 km).

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Madridejos?
A: The routes in Madridejos are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an impressive average score of 4.8 stars from over 50 reviews. Hikers often praise the varied terrain, from coastal paths to rolling hills, and the serene environment. Over 270 hikers have used komoot to explore the area, indicating its popularity for outdoor activities.

Q: Are there any moderate hiking trails for a bit more challenge?
A: Yes, Madridejos has 13 moderate trails for those looking for a slightly more challenging experience. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1018339598/senda-del-lince-circular-to-the-castrola-cave-pr-to-11" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Senda del Lince – circular to the Castrola cave (PR-TO 11)&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate route, spanning 8.6 miles (13.9 km) with an elevation gain of over 260 meters. Another good moderate option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20128805" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente del Umbrión – Castrola Cave loop from El Fraile&lt;/a&gt;, which is 6.3 miles (10.2 km) long.

Q: What is the best time of year to visit Madridejos for hiking?
A: Madridejos, being on Bantayan Island, generally offers a pleasant climate for outdoor activities. The dry season, typically from November to May, is often considered the best time for hiking, as it provides more stable weather conditions and less humidity. This allows for comfortable exploration of coastal areas, mangrove gardens, and inland rolling terrain.

Q: Are there any lakes or water features to explore near the hiking trails?
A: While Madridejos is known for its coastal beauty, there are also several lakes in the surrounding region that can be explored. Notable lakes include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5421216" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna Grande de Villafranca&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5878870" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de Peñahueca&lt;/a&gt;. These offer different natural scenery compared to the coastal walks and mangrove areas.

Q: What kind of views can I expect on the trails?
A: Madridejos offers diverse scenic views. Coastal walks provide expansive vistas of the Visayan Sea, often with glimpses of Iloilo on the horizon. Inland trails traverse open grasslands and second-growth forests, offering serene natural landscapes. Elevated points like the watch tower at Kota Park or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3902682" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Calderico Hill&lt;/a&gt; provide panoramic views of the surrounding area and coastline.
tips: Path just 500 meters from the Castrola Cave.
